inputs.reasoning: Named capture groups exercise the parser's named group handling and the compiler's capture group tracking. This exercises special syntax parsing in the group context.
Nested repetitions stress the parser's repetition handling and compiler's optimization/simplification logic. This is a classic case that needs careful handling to avoid catastrophic backtracking.
Negated character classes with ranges exercise the character class parser and the builder's range inversion logic. The underscore and ranges test boundary conditions.
Counted repetitions with various bounds (exact, range, open-ended) exercise the counted repetition parser and compiler's handling of min/max constraints.
Non-capturing groups with alternation test the parser's alternation handling and the compiler's optimization of alternation nodes.
Character class escapes (\w, \d, \s) with repetition exercise escape sequence parsing and character class construction with standard classes.
Anchors (^ and $) combined with dot-star pattern exercise anchor parsing and the compiler's handling of anchored patterns and wildcards.
Deeply nested capturing groups test the parser's recursion handling and the compiler's simplification of nested concatenations.
Multiple character classes with different ranges and repetitions exercise the parser's sequential class handling and the compiler's character class compilation.
Word boundaries (\b) combined with word character class (\w) exercise boundary condition parsing and the compiler's special handling of word boundaries.
